subject: Repeated keychain prompts for mail or Wi-Fi password
description: User asked to approve keychain access many times per day for Mail or network login.

ts_steps.title: Unlock login keychain
ts_steps.action: Keychain Access → login keychain — if locked, unlock with login password.

ts_steps.title: Search duplicates
ts_steps.action: Keychain Access search for SSID or mail server hostname; delete obvious duplicate password items after identifying correct one (export first if unsure).

ts_steps.title: Mail re-auth
ts_steps.action: Mail → Settings → Accounts — remove account if allowed, quit Mail, add account fresh, save password when prompted Always Allow.

ts_steps.title: Wi-Fi re-save
ts_steps.action: Forget SSID, rejoin, enter password once, confirm Remember.

ts_steps.title: iCloud Keychain toggle
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Apple ID → iCloud → Passwords and Keychain — brief off/on with user awareness of sync pause.

ts_steps.title: Third-party password apps
ts_steps.action: Disable browser password manager extensions temporarily to test conflicts.

ts_steps.title: Login password change mismatch
ts_steps.action: If password recently changed, user may need to update keychain password when macOS prompts at login.

ts_steps.title: MDM check
ts_steps.action: If keychain controls grayed, escalate MDM restriction.

ts_steps.title: New keychain last resort
ts_steps.action: After backup of critical items, create new default keychain per Apple doc — disruptive, schedule with user.

internal_information: Duplicates, unlock state, post-password-change mismatch, MDM. No legacy Keychain First Aid UI dependency.
